tactics.name: rfl
tactics.effect: Close a goal `a = a` (definitional equality).
tactics.example: example : 42 = 42 := by rfl

tactics.name: exact
tactics.effect: Close the goal with the given term.
tactics.example: example (h : P) : P := by exact h

tactics.name: exact?
tactics.effect: Search the library for a closing term and suggest it.

tactics.name: assumption
tactics.effect: Close the goal with a matching hypothesis from the context.
tactics.example: example (h : P) : P := by assumption

tactics.name: decide
tactics.effect: Prove decidable propositions by computation.
tactics.example: example : 2 + 3 = 5 := by decide

tactics.name: trivial
tactics.effect: Try `rfl`, `assumption`, `contradiction`, and similar.
tactics.example: example : True := by trivial

tactics.name: contradiction
tactics.effect: Close the goal from conflicting hypotheses.
tactics.example: example (h : False) : P := by contradiction

groups.title: Rewriting
groups.description: Transform the goal or hypotheses using equalities and rules.

tactics.name: rw
tactics.effect: Rewrite using an equality or iff; `rw [← h]` for reverse; `rw [...] at k` targets a hypothesis.
tactics.example: example (h : a = b) : a + 1 = b + 1 := by rw [h]

tactics.name: nth_rw
tactics.effect: Rewrite only the n-th occurrence.
tactics.example: example (h : a = b) : a + a = a + b := by nth_rw 2 [h]

tactics.name: simp
tactics.effect: Repeatedly apply rewrite rules from a configurable set; `simp only [...]` for explicit control.
tactics.example: example : 0 + n = n := by simp

tactics.name: push_neg
tactics.effect: Push negation inward through connectives and quantifiers.
tactics.example: example (h : ¬∀ x, P x) : ∃ x, ¬P x := by push_neg at h; exact h

tactics.name: norm_num
tactics.effect: Evaluate closed numerical expressions.
tactics.example: example : (2 : ℤ) + 3 = 5 := by norm_num

tactics.name: ring
tactics.effect: Prove polynomial equalities in commutative (semi)rings.
tactics.example: example (a b : ℤ) : (a + b)^2 = a^2 + 2*a*b + b^2 := by ring

groups.title: Goal Structuring
groups.description: Reshape the goal or introduce intermediate steps.

tactics.name: intro
tactics.effect: Assume the antecedent of `→` or `∀`, adding it as a hypothesis.
tactics.example: example : P → P := by intro h; exact h

tactics.name: revert
tactics.effect: Move a hypothesis back into the goal (inverse of `intro`).
tactics.example: example (h : P) : P := by revert h; intro h; exact h

tactics.name: apply
tactics.effect: Backward reasoning: reduce the goal using a lemma or hypothesis.
tactics.example: example (f : P → Q) (p : P) : Q := by apply f; exact p

tactics.name: have
tactics.effect: Introduce a new fact derived from existing ones.
tactics.example: example (f : P → Q) (p : P) : Q := by have q := f p; exact q

tactics.name: suffices
tactics.effect: Like `have`, but prove the main goal first.
tactics.example: example (f : P → Q) (p : P) : Q := by suffices P by exact f this; exact p

tactics.name: refine
tactics.effect: Like `exact` but allows `?_` placeholders that become new goals.
tactics.example: example (f : P → Q) (p : P) : Q := by refine f ?_; exact p

tactics.name: calc
tactics.effect: Chain equalities or inequalities through intermediate steps.
tactics.example: example : 1 + 1 = 2 := by calc 1 + 1 = 2 := by norm_num

tactics.name: symm
tactics.effect: Swap sides of an equality or iff.
tactics.example: example (h : a = b) : b = a := by symm; exact h

tactics.name: trans
tactics.effect: Chain two relations through an intermediate value.
tactics.example: example (h₁ : a = b) (h₂ : b = c) : a = c := by trans b <;> assumption

groups.title: Case Analysis and Induction
groups.description: Split goals or hypotheses by structure.

tactics.name: constructor
tactics.effect: Split `P ∧ Q` or `P ↔ Q` into subgoals.
tactics.example: example (p : P) (q : Q) : P ∧ Q := by constructor <;> assumption

tactics.name: left / right
tactics.effect: Choose which side of `P ∨ Q` to prove.
tactics.example: example (p : P) : P ∨ Q := by left; exact p

tactics.name: obtain
tactics.effect: Destructure a hypothesis: `obtain ⟨a, b⟩ := h`.
tactics.example: example (h : P ∧ Q) : Q := by obtain ⟨_, q⟩ := h; exact q

tactics.name: cases
tactics.effect: Case analysis on a hypothesis.
tactics.example:
example (h : P ∨ Q) : Q ∨ P := by cases h with
  | inl p => right; exact p
  | inr q => left; exact q

tactics.name: rcases
tactics.effect: Recursive pattern matching on hypotheses.
tactics.example: example (h : P ∧ (Q ∨ R)) : True := by rcases h with ⟨_, _ | _⟩ <;> trivial

tactics.name: rintro
tactics.effect: Combine `intro` and `rcases`.
tactics.example: example : P ∧ Q → Q ∧ P := by rintro ⟨p, q⟩; exact ⟨q, p⟩

tactics.name: induction
tactics.effect: Structural induction on an inductive type.
tactics.example:
example (n : ℕ) : 0 + n = n := by induction n with
  | zero => rfl
  | succ n ih => rw [Nat.add_succ, ih]

tactics.name: by_contra
tactics.effect: Assume `¬ goal` and derive `False` (classical).
tactics.example: example : P ∨ ¬P := by by_contra h; push_neg at h; exact h.2 h.1

tactics.name: by_cases
tactics.effect: Split into `P` and `¬P` branches (classical).
tactics.example: example : P ∨ ¬P := by by_cases h : P <;> [left; right] <;> exact h

tactics.name: exfalso
tactics.effect: Change the goal to `False`.
tactics.example: example (h : False) : P := by exfalso; exact h

groups.title: Existentials and Extensionality
groups.description: Supply witnesses or prove equality by components.

tactics.name: use
tactics.effect: Supply a witness for an existential goal.
tactics.example: example : ∃ n : ℕ, n = 2 := by use 2

tactics.name: choose
tactics.effect: Extract a witness function from `∀ x, ∃ y, P x y`.
tactics.example: example (h : ∀ x, ∃ y, P x y) : ... := by choose f hf using h

tactics.name: ext
tactics.effect: Prove `f = g` by showing `f x = g x` for arbitrary `x`.
tactics.example: example : (fun x : ℕ => x) = id := by ext x; rfl

groups.title: Automation
groups.description: Higher-level tactics that search for or compute proofs.

tactics.name: tauto
tactics.effect: Close propositional tautologies.
tactics.example: example (h : P ∧ Q) : Q ∧ P := by tauto

tactics.name: omega
tactics.effect: Decision procedure for linear integer/natural arithmetic.
tactics.example: example (h : n < m) : n + 1 ≤ m := by omega

tactics.name: linarith
tactics.effect: Prove goals from linear arithmetic over ordered fields.
tactics.example: example (h₁ : a ≤ b) (h₂ : b < c) : a < c := by linarith

tactics.name: grind
tactics.effect: SMT-style solver handling quantifiers, congruence, and arithmetic.
tactics.example: example (h : a = b) (h₂ : b = c) : a = c := by grind

tactics.name: apply?
tactics.effect: Search for a lemma that makes progress and suggest it.

groups.title: Combinators
groups.description: Modify how other tactics are applied.

tactics.name: all_goals
tactics.effect: Run a tactic on every open goal.
tactics.example: example : P ∧ P := by constructor; all_goals assumption

tactics.name: repeat
tactics.effect: Apply a tactic until it fails.
tactics.example: example : P ∧ P := by constructor; repeat assumption

tactics.name: try
tactics.effect: Attempt a tactic; succeed silently if it fails.
tactics.example: example : P := by try rfl; assumption

tactics.name: first
tactics.effect: Try tactics in order: `first | t₁ | t₂`.
tactics.example: example : P := by first | rfl | assumption

tactics.effect: Apply a tactic to all goals produced by the previous tactic.
tactics.example: example (p : P) (q : Q) : P ∧ Q := by constructor <;> assumption
